pests.name: Leaf Miner
pests.impact: Adult females puncture leaves for feeding and egg-laying, causing white spots and leaf drying due to water evaporation. Larvae feed inside leaves, creating thin white tunnels, reducing photosynthesis. Wounds allow secondary infections.
pests.solution: Regular field inspection. Natural biological control by Hymenoptera species (Diglyphas isaea, Hemiptersenus semiabiclavus, Opius Spp) can be promoted by placing discarded leaves from harvested crops in wooden cages or on white polythene sheets to breed parasitoids, then releasing them. Yellow polythene traps with grease. Using butterfly nets in the morning when leaf miners are active. Erecting a 5-foot high net around the field before cultivation. Deep plowing to destroy weeds and pupae. Removing old infected leaves and burying damaged leaves. Avoiding infected seedlings from nurseries. Chemical control: Neem extract (640g/16L) for early stages. If infestation is severe: Azadiractin 1% EC (16ml/16L), Abamectin 18g/L EC (9.6ml/16L).

pests.name: Cutworm
pests.impact: Larvae are active at night, cutting young plants at the base. Damage is more prevalent in dry weather. High larval populations during root development can damage roots, reducing market value.
pests.solution: Deep plowing exposes larvae and pupae to predatory birds. Good weed control and field sanitation. Regular field inspection to hand-pick hidden larvae when populations are low. If damage is severe, apply recommended insecticides immediately after planting: Profenophos 500g/LEC (32ML/16L), Etofenprox 100g/LEC (24ML/16L).

pests.name: Root-Knot Nematode
pests.impact: Symptoms not visible at low populations. High populations cause yellowing, stunting, wilting, and uneven growth. Examination of roots shows galls. Root forking, deformed roots, and hairy roots can also be seen.
pests.solution: Maintain uninfected fields. Clean tools used in infected fields before using in uninfected ones. Prevent entry of animals and water from infected fields. For infected fields: Deep plowing and exposing soil to sunlight for several days. Crop rotation. Applying chicken manure two weeks before planting in addition to other organic fertilizers.

pests.name: Snails And Slugs
pests.impact: Eat young shoots during germination, potentially destroying the entire crop. Damage is common during rainy seasons.
pests.solution: Improve field sanitation and weed control. Remove hiding places during the day. Inspect fields at night and hand-pick. Place baits. Apply recommended molluscicides: Metaldehyde 3% RB (25.0 kg/ha), Metaldehyde 4% RB (25.0 kg/ha), Metaldehyde 5% GR (25.0 kg/ha).

diseases.name: Blight
diseases.solution: Use healthy planting material. Keep the field clean. Use recommended fungicides (Mancozeb, Maneb).

diseases.name: Soft Rot
diseases.solution: Keep the field clean. Improve field drainage.
